name: Carnival (Trinidad)
traditions: J'ouvert (dawn street party); mas (masquerade) bands; soca and calypso music competitions; steel pan orchestras; costume parades.
historicalBackground: Trinidad Carnival emerged from enslaved Africans' Canboulay celebrations in the 18th century, blending with French Creole masquerade traditions.
description: The Caribbean's greatest cultural showcase, featuring explosive soca music, elaborate masquerade costumes, and the iconic J'ouvert street party.
